hero.subtitle: Digital agency for Russian-speaking business owners in the US, Canada, and Europe. EU-based business in Bucharest, Romania. Websites, mobile apps, ads, and SEO — built to American standards, priced for real businesses.

trust.label: Trusted
trust.title: Trusted by teams across Europe & North America
trust.subtitle: From bootstrapped founders in Brooklyn to growing businesses in Berlin — same care, same standards in 50+ countries.

why_us_diaspora.label: Why us
why_us_diaspora.title: Built for Russian-speaking entrepreneurs abroad.
why_us_diaspora.subtitle: Most agencies charge you for an office you'll never visit and a process designed for Fortune 500. We're a small EU-based team that gets your context.

items.title: We speak your language — literally.
items.body: Discuss the brief in Russian or Ukrainian. No nuance lost in translation, no cultural mismatch. Final delivery in any language your customers speak.

items.title: EU-registered, GDPR-compliant.
items.body: Bucharest-based business in Romania. Legitimate invoices, EU contracts, banking that works with US/Canadian payment systems. No grey-zone freelancer surprises.

items.title: Time zones that work for you.
items.body: UTC+2 (Romania) overlaps comfortably with European mornings and North American evenings. You'll get same-day replies during your business hours, not three days later.

items.title: Western quality, ex-USSR pricing.
items.body: Same quality bar as Manhattan or London agencies — without their $5K/month office overhead. Fair, transparent prices that make sense for a real business, not a venture-backed unicorn.

about_preview.label: About
about_preview.title: One owner. Direct accountability. No middlemen.
about_preview.body: Alexey Kachan founded the agency in 2014 after a decade in-house at product companies. Every project still goes across his desk — strategy, design reviews, code reviews, ad reports. You get senior judgment on every call, not a junior with a template.

process.label: Process

process.subtitle: A proven framework that turns ideas into measurable business outcomes.

launch.title: Launch & Scale

differentiators.label: Why us
differentiators.title: Four reasons clients stay for years
differentiators.subtitle: Most agencies sell deliverables. We sell outcomes — and we put that on paper.

single_owner.title: Single accountable owner
single_owner.description: One name on every email, every report, every line of code. No account-manager telephone game.

fast_ship.title: Ship in weeks, not quarters
fast_ship.description: Tight scopes, written specs, weekly demos. Most projects launch in 4–8 weeks.

measurable.title: Numbers, not opinions
measurable.description: We define success metrics before the kickoff. Every report ties back to revenue, leads, or rankings.

long_term.title: Long-term partner, not vendor
long_term.description: Average client relationship is 4+ years. We invest in your business because we plan to be there.

items.quote: Our Google Ads spend dropped 38% while leads grew 2.4× in six months. He explains every decision in plain language and the numbers always check out.
items.author: Sofia Russo

items.quote: Worked with three agencies before — Alexey is on a different planet. He cares about the business, not the deliverable. We've been with him four years.
items.author: Daniel Cohen

blog_teaser.label: Journal
blog_teaser.title: Latest thinking
blog_teaser.subtitle: Working notes on web performance, SEO, and ad strategy — short reads, no buzzwords.

faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers to common questions. Don't see yours? Just ask.

items.question: How long does a typical project take?
items.answer: Most marketing sites launch in 4–6 weeks. Larger builds — multilingual, e-commerce, or custom integrations — usually run 8–12 weeks. We share a written timeline with milestones before the contract is signed.

items.question: What does it cost?
items.answer: Website projects start at $395 for a focused landing page and $1,195 for a 5–15 page business site. Mobile apps from $2,495 for trainer, course, or booking apps. Ads management is a flat monthly retainer (from $1,195/month). SEO engagements start at $395 for an audit, $715/month for ongoing work. Every quote is fixed — no surprise invoices.

items.question: Do you work in my language?
items.answer: We deliver in English, Ukrainian, and Russian. All copy is reviewed by native speakers, not machine-translated.

items.question: Who actually does the work?
items.answer: Alexey leads strategy, design, and code review on every project. A small team of trusted specialists handles execution. No outsourcing chains, no hidden subcontractors.

items.question: What about ongoing support?
items.answer: We offer monthly support plans starting at $79/month — security updates, performance monitoring, content edits, and analytics reviews. Most clients stay on after launch.

items.question: Can you take over a project from another agency?
items.answer: Yes. We've inherited dozens of half-finished projects. We start with an audit, share a recovery plan, and migrate cleanly with zero downtime.

final_cta.label: Let's talk
final_cta.title: Ready to outgrow your competition?
final_cta.subtitle: Tell me about your project — I respond within 4 hours during business hours.

about_page.h1: Built by one operator. Backed by twelve years.
about_page.lead: Alexey Kachan is a digital agency with a single owner accountable for every line of code, every design decision, and every line item on every report. Founded 2014, working worldwide from Ukraine.

story.label: Story
story.title: Why a single-operator agency, in 2026
story.body_1: Most digital agencies are organized like factories — a sales team, an account team, a strategy team, a design team, a development team, a media-buying team. The client pitches a project to one team, signs with another, gets execution from a third, and reports back to a fourth. Every handoff is a translation, and every translation loses information.
story.body_2: After ten years in-house at product companies — leading web teams, running paid acquisition, managing SEO programs — I started Alexey Kachan Agency in 2014 because I wanted the opposite. One person responsible. The strategist is also the senior code reviewer. The designer is also the SEO lead. The person on the kickoff call is the person on the post-launch call eighteen months later.
story.body_3: It scales by being deliberate. We do not take every project that lands in the inbox. We pair with a small bench of trusted specialists — senior engineers, native-speaker copywriters, ad-platform specialists — when scope demands. But every relationship runs through one accountable owner. That is the product. It is also the moat.

items.label: Years building digital products
items.body: Started in-house at SaaS companies in 2012. Founded the agency in 2014.

items.label: Engagements delivered
items.body: Web builds, redesigns, SEO retainers, paid-media management. Most ran 6 to 24 months.

items.label: Countries served
items.body: Active client work across DACH, France, Spain, Italy, Romania, UK, US, Canada, Ukraine.

items.label: Average client relationship
items.body: Most clients renew. Several have been with us since the agency's first year.

values.label: Values
values.title: Five things we will not do

items.title: Surprise pricing
items.body: Every quote is fixed before the contract is signed. If scope changes, we re-quote in writing — never silently.

items.title: Vanity metrics
items.body: If a chart does not connect to revenue, leads, or rankings, it does not go in the report.

items.title: Outsourcing chains
items.body: When we use specialists, we name them. Your project is never quietly handed to a third agency.

items.title: Machine-translated content
items.body: Every word in a non-English language is reviewed by a native speaker. Period.

items.title: Locked-in vendor traps
items.body: Codebase on your GitHub, content on your CMS, ad accounts on your domains. Cancel anytime.

person.name: Alexey Kachan
person.bio_short: Twelve years building digital products. Ten in-house, then independent since 2014. Strong opinions about engineering hygiene, SEO, and saying no to bad scope.

about_page.cta_title: Want this kind of accountability on your project?

contacts_page.lead: We respond within 4 hours during European business hours. Most projects start with a 30-minute discovery call before any quote — it costs nothing and ends with a clear answer either way.
contacts_page.form_subtitle: 5 fields. Two minutes. We'll come back with a fixed scope and price.
contacts_page.form_success_body: We've got your message and will reply within 4 hours during business hours. In the meantime, you can browse recent work or read the journal.
contacts_page.form_error: Something went wrong sending the message. Please try again or email us directly.

direct.label: Or reach us directly
direct.phone_note: Romania office — phone coming soon. Use email or messengers for fastest response.

contacts_page.channels_subtitle: Pick the channel that fits your question.

channel_notes.whatsapp: For quick questions. < 1hr response in business hours.

common.success_body: Thanks. We'll review and come back within 4 hours during business hours with a written scope, timeline and price.
common.intro_note: Answer only what's relevant — drafts auto-save in your browser. Skip any section that doesn't apply.

website.lead: The more specific your answers, the more accurate our quote will be. Skip sections that don't apply — we'll fill the gaps in the kickoff call.

sections.title: Company & geography
sections.subtitle: A 30-second snapshot helps us match the right tone and time-zone.
sections.name: section_company
sections.placeholder:
• Year founded, brief history
• Decision maker if different from contact (name, contact)
• Existing site URL and social links
• City / region / country / international
• Time-zone for calls

sections.title: Business & product
sections.subtitle: Helps us pick the right type of site — landing, catalogue, e-commerce, portal.
sections.name: section_business
sections.placeholder:
• Describe the business in 2–3 sentences (as you would to a neighbour)
• What you sell: product / service / subscription / SaaS / info-product
• Top 5–10 products or categories
• Average ticket / price range
• Margin level (low / medium / high)
• Seasonality — when are the peaks?
• Sales cycle — hours, days, weeks, months?

sections.title: Target audience
sections.subtitle: Defines the tone, the trust elements and the catalogue filters.
sections.name: section_audience
sections.placeholder:
• B2B / B2C / B2G / mixed
• 2–4 audience segments — age, role, income, geography
• What problem your product solves for each segment
• Customer language — professional terms / plain / premium
• Top objections and fears
• What matters most when choosing — price / speed / quality / service / guarantees / reputation (rank them)

sections.title: Competitors & positioning
sections.subtitle: Your USP is the foundation of every page and CTA.
sections.name: section_competitors
sections.placeholder:
• 3–5 direct competitors (links)
• What they do well that you like
• What they do badly that you don't want
• Your real USP (not 'quality and price')
• 3–5 reasons to buy from you specifically
• Weak points you'd like the design or content to compensate for

sections.title: Goals & KPIs
sections.subtitle: Goals determine everything — structure, CTA placement, tracking.
sections.name: section_goals
sections.placeholder:
• Top 1–2 goals: leads / online sales / catalogue / image / database building / customer support
• Target action: form / call / purchase / subscription / download / signup
• Measurable KPI: leads or sales per month
• Where customers come from today and roughly how many

sections.title: Site structure
sections.subtitle: Used to estimate scope, choose the CMS, design URL architecture.
sections.name: section_structure
sections.placeholder:
• Pages / sections you need (or ask us to suggest)
• Catalogue depth — categories, sub-categories, items
• Blog / news / articles needed?
• Service-specific landing pages, city or segment pages?
• Languages — which, which is master?
• Multi-region — sub-domains / folders / geo-routing?

sections.title: Functionality needed
sections.subtitle: Each item is hours or days of work and shapes the tech stack.
sections.name: section_functionality
sections.placeholder:
Tick or describe what's needed:
• Catalogue with filters — which filters?
• Cart, checkout, online payments — which methods?
• Customer area / login (email / phone / SSO)
• Calculators, configurator, online booking
• Live chat / chat-bot
• Forms — which, where do they go?
• Integrations — CRM (which?), 1C, ERP, telephony, email tools, analytics, messengers, payments, delivery
• Search, comparison, wishlist, reviews, loyalty, multi-user mode (B2B)

sections.title: Content readiness
sections.subtitle: Content is the #1 cause of timeline slips — important to know upfront.
sections.name: section_content
sections.placeholder:
• Who writes the copy: you / our copywriter / together
• Photo & video: in-house shoot / stock / new shoot by us
• Logo + brand guidelines ready? (please share)
• Brand colours, fonts, graphics in place?
• Number of products/services to describe at launch
• Texts ready, or to be written from scratch?

sections.title: Design preferences
sections.subtitle: Speeds up design and reduces revision rounds.
sections.name: section_design
sections.placeholder:
• Style: minimal / classic / premium / bold / techy / warm / corporate / other
• 3–5 sites you love (links + what specifically)
• 2–3 sites you don't like (links + why)
• Colour palette — keep brand / open to suggestions / colours to avoid
• Tone — friendly / expert / formal / playful
• Dark mode?
• Accessibility (WCAG) needs?
• Animation level — minimal / moderate / rich

sections.title: Technical requirements
sections.subtitle: Determines stack and any sub-vendors.
sections.name: section_tech
sections.placeholder:
• CMS / platform preference: WordPress / Shopify / Webflow / custom / no preference
• Hosting — your existing / our recommendation
• Domain — you have it / need to register
• Admin panel for self-editing — which blocks editable?
• Expected traffic per month
• Adaptive — desktop + tablet + mobile (priority?)
• SEO — schema, sitemap, robots, metas — handled by us?
• Analytics — GA4 / Plausible / Clarity / other
• CRM data sync, call-tracking
• Security — SSL, anti-bot, backups, GDPR / data-residency

sections.title: SEO & growth
sections.subtitle: If SEO is planned, structure and URLs need to support it from day one.
sections.name: section_seo
sections.placeholder:
• SEO promotion planned after launch?
• Keyword research ready / to be done
• Target regions
• SEO-driven structure needed (cluster pages)?
• Existing site with accumulated SEO equity? (we'll handle 301s)

sections.title: Timeline & budget
sections.subtitle: Realistic scope. Better to ship less, well, than everything badly.
sections.name: section_timeline
sections.placeholder:
• Desired launch date
• Hard deadline reason (event, season, contract)
• Build budget range — from / to
• Post-launch support budget per month
• Willing to invest in paid traffic post-launch — rough monthly?

sections.title: Post-launch support
sections.subtitle: Sets expectations on who runs the site after launch.
sections.name: section_support
sections.placeholder:
• Who'll own day-to-day support: you / us / hired developer
• Need admin training and video docs?
• SLA — incident response time?
• Regular feature updates planned?

sections.title: Legal & operational
sections.subtitle: Avoid surprises before signing the contract.
sections.name: section_legal
sections.placeholder:
• Code & design ownership transfer terms
• Payment milestones preferences
• Approvers — DM, designer, marketer
• Preferred comms — Telegram / email / Slack / Trello
• Reporting cadence and format

ads.lead: Skip what doesn't apply — drafts save in your browser. We'll come back with a recommended channel mix, a starting budget, and a measurable target before any contract.

sections.title: Company & geography
sections.subtitle: Helps us match channel choices to your market.
sections.name: section_company
sections.placeholder:
• Website URL and social handles
• Decision maker for creatives & strategy if different from contact
• Cities / countries you advertise in
• Time-zone

sections.title: Product & offer
sections.subtitle: Defines target CPA, campaign type and creative angle.
sections.name: section_product
sections.placeholder:
• What we're advertising — single product / category / whole business
• Price / average order value / price range
• Margin (rough %) — drives allowable CPL
• LTV if known — does paid pay back over time?
• Unique selling points
• Current offer (discount / bonus / free audit / trial)
• Lead magnet, if any

sections.title: Target audience
sections.subtitle: Drives channel selection, targeting, creative direction.
sections.name: section_audience
sections.placeholder:
• 2–4 segments — demographics, geography, interests, behaviour, income
• Pains and needs per segment
• Funnel stage — unaware / problem-aware / solution-aware / ready to buy
• Objections and fears
• Where they hang out (social, search, sites)

sections.title: Goals & KPI
sections.subtitle: Goal determines bidding strategy and definition of success.
sections.name: section_goals
sections.placeholder:
Pick 1–2 main goals:
• Leads (forms, calls)
• Direct online sales
• App installs
• Subscriptions / database
• Awareness / reach
• Site traffic without explicit conversion
• Re-engagement / remarketing

KPI:
• Target CPA / cost-per-customer
• Target ROAS
• Target lead/sales volume per month
• Time to hit those targets

sections.title: Budget
sections.subtitle: Defines reach, number of testable hypotheses, channels we can run.
sections.name: section_budget
sections.placeholder:
• Monthly ad budget total
• Channel split if you have a view, or ask us to recommend
• Test period and test budget
• Willing to scale once KPI is hit?
• VAT and agency fee — inside the budget or on top?

sections.title: Channels
sections.subtitle: Channel mix is matched to goal and audience.
sections.name: section_channels
sections.placeholder:
Tick interesting / ask us to recommend:
• Google Ads (Search, PMax, Display, YouTube)
• Meta Ads (Facebook + Instagram)
• TikTok Ads
• LinkedIn Ads
• Microsoft / Bing Ads
• Telegram Ads
• YouTube influencer marketing
• Marketplace ads (Amazon, eBay)
• Local maps (Google Business)
• Email campaigns
• Programmatic / DSP

sections.title: History & analytics
sections.subtitle: Without data, optimisation is guesswork. If gaps exist, we fix them first.
sections.name: section_history
sections.placeholder:
• Previous ads run — where, results, what worked / didn't
• Ad-account access ready or to be created
• Pixels installed: GA4, Meta Pixel, TikTok, etc.
• Goals/events configured (which)
• Call-tracking — provider, status
• End-to-end analytics — provider
• CRM and API access
• Customer data for lookalikes / retargeting

sections.title: Landing pages
sections.subtitle: Bad landings burn budget. We may need to fix them first.
sections.name: section_landing
sections.placeholder:
• Where does traffic land — homepage / category / dedicated landing / quiz / form / messenger / call
• Landing pages ready or need to be built
• Mobile version quality
• Page speed (Core Web Vitals) — known?
• Steps from click to target action
• Current site conversion rate (if known)

sections.title: Creatives & assets
sections.subtitle: Creative production is its own line item — don't underestimate it.
sections.name: section_creatives
sections.placeholder:
• Who produces creatives — you / us / together
• Copy, headlines, offers ready?
• Photo and video material — what, what quality
• Brand guidelines and style
• Tone — serious / friendly / provocative / expert
• Visual or text restrictions (legal, corporate)
• Topics / angles you don't want to use

sections.title: USP & messages
sections.subtitle: Drives creative themes and ad copy hierarchy.
sections.name: section_usp
sections.placeholder:
• 3–5 key messages we want to land
• How you differ from competitors in ads
• Triggers that work — price / urgency / exclusivity / social proof / guarantee / expertise
• Promotions and special offers planned (when, what, duration)

sections.title: Competitor ads
sections.subtitle: Benchmark for bids, creatives, offers.
sections.name: section_competitor_ads
sections.placeholder:
• 3–5 competitors you see advertising
• What's strong about their ads
• Their offers and prices
• Where they advertise most actively

sections.title: Legal & vertical restrictions
sections.subtitle: Moderation can kill a campaign. Knowing in advance saves time.
sections.name: section_legal_restrictions
sections.placeholder:
• Regulated vertical? (medical, finance, supplements, gambling, alcohol, tobacco, pharma, education, real estate, investments)
• Required licences, certificates, disclaimers
• Ad-disclosure / labelling requirements (who handles)
• Age restrictions
• Legal review needed for creatives?

sections.title: Seasonality & priorities
sections.subtitle: Helps allocate budget across months and product lines.
sections.name: section_seasonality
sections.placeholder:
• Product seasonality (peaks/lows by month)
• Priority products / cities / regions for the next 3 months
• Areas you don't want to advertise
• Sales-team hours (when to show 'call now' CTAs)

sections.title: Sales team & lead handling
sections.subtitle: The best ads are useless if leads aren't worked. Sometimes we fix sales first.
sections.name: section_sales
sections.placeholder:
• Who receives the leads and how fast
• How many sales reps process them
• Hours / time-zone
• Sales scripts and process docs
• Lead-to-sale conversion rate (if known)
• Where leads are stored — CRM / spreadsheet / email
• Are leads being lost or processed slowly?

sections.title: Timeline & reporting
sections.subtitle: Aligns expectations on cadence and visibility.
sections.name: section_timeline
sections.placeholder:
• Desired campaign start date
• Test period length (typically 1–2 months)
• Reporting format and cadence — weekly / monthly / live dashboard
• Key metrics you want to see in reports
• Preferred comms channel

privacy.lead: We respect your privacy and treat any data you share with us as a working tool — never an asset. This policy explains what we collect, why, and what your rights are.

sections.title: Who we are
sections.body: Alexey Kachan Agency ("we", "us") is a sole-proprietorship digital agency based in Ukraine, operating the website alexeykachan.com. The data controller is Alexey Kachan. You can reach us at info@alexeykachan.com for any data-related question.

sections.title: What we collect
sections.body: When you fill in a form on this site (contact, brief, newsletter), we collect the fields you provide — name, email, company, project details. When you visit any page, our hosting (Cloudflare) logs your IP address for security and rate-limiting purposes. With your consent, we run privacy-respecting analytics (Plausible / Microsoft Clarity) that record anonymized session data.

sections.title: Why we collect it
sections.body: Form data: to respond to your inquiry and quote your project. We do not use it for marketing without your explicit opt-in. Server logs: to detect abuse and keep the site online. Analytics: to understand which content is useful and what we should write more of.

sections.title: Who we share it with
sections.body: We do not sell or rent your data. We share form submissions only with the third-party processors needed to deliver the service: Web3Forms (form delivery), Cloudflare (hosting + email routing), Google Workspace (email).

sections.title: How long we keep it
sections.body: Form submissions: 24 months from the last interaction, then automatically deleted unless they're tied to an active engagement. Newsletter subscriptions: until you unsubscribe (one click in every email). Server logs: 30 days, then automatically rotated.

sections.title: Your rights
sections.body: You can request a copy of your data, ask us to correct it, or ask us to delete it — at any time, by emailing info@alexeykachan.com. We respond within 30 days. If you're an EU/UK resident, you also have the right to lodge a complaint with your local data-protection authority.

sections.title: Cookies
sections.body: We set technical cookies needed for the site to work (language preference, session). Analytics cookies are loaded only with your consent — you can change your choice anytime from the cookie banner. See the Cookie Policy for the full list.

sections.title: Changes to this policy
sections.body: We may update this policy as the agency grows or as the law changes. The effective date at the top of this page reflects the latest version. We will not retroactively reduce your protections without notifying affected contacts.

cookies.lead: We use cookies in three categories: essential (always on), analytics (with consent), and marketing (with consent). Here is the full list and what each one does.

sections.title: Essential cookies
sections.body: Required for the site to work. They store your language preference, your cookie-consent choice, and a session identifier for forms. They do not track you across sites and cannot be disabled. Lifetime: 12 months. Provider: Alexey Kachan Agency.

sections.title: Analytics (with consent)
sections.body: Plausible Analytics or Microsoft Clarity. Used to understand which pages are most visited, which articles are read end-to-end, and where people get stuck. Anonymized — no IP storage, no fingerprinting. You can opt out from the cookie banner at any time. Lifetime: 12 months.

sections.title: Marketing (with consent)
sections.body: Loaded only on pages where we run experiments — usually a Meta Pixel or Google Ads conversion tag. Used to measure the effectiveness of ad campaigns we run for our own brand. Off by default. Lifetime: 13 months. You can opt out from the cookie banner at any time.

terms.lead: These are the terms that apply when you visit alexeykachan.com or engage us for a project. Project-specific contracts always supersede these terms — they exist for the website itself.

sections.title: Use of the site
sections.body: You may read, download, and share content from this site for personal or professional reference. You may not republish substantial portions of it without permission. Code samples in articles are MIT-licensed unless otherwise noted.

sections.title: Engagements
sections.body: If we agree to work together, the engagement is governed by a separate written agreement signed by both parties — typically a Statement of Work covering scope, deliverables, payment terms, and IP. The website is not itself a contract.

sections.title: Disclaimers
sections.body: Articles on this site reflect our opinion as of their publication date. Marketing and SEO are probability games — we share what works for our clients, but no outcome is guaranteed without scope, timeline, and budget agreed in writing.

sections.title: Liability
sections.body: alexeykachan.com is provided "as is". We are not liable for any business decision you make based on content alone. Our liability under any signed engagement is capped per the terms in that agreement.

sections.title: Governing law
sections.body: These terms are governed by Ukrainian law. Engagement agreements may specify a different governing law per the client's preference; this is negotiated as part of the contract.

impressum.lead: Diese Anbieterkennzeichnung wird zur Erfüllung der Informationspflichten nach § 5 TMG für unsere DACH-Kunden bereitgestellt — auch wenn die Agentur in der Ukraine ansässig ist.

sections.body: Trotz sorgfältiger inhaltlicher Kontrolle übernehmen wir keine Haftung für die Inhalte externer Links. Für den Inhalt der verlinkten Seiten sind ausschließlich deren Betreiber verantwortlich.

sections.title: Urheberrecht
sections.body: Alle Inhalte dieser Website (Texte, Bilder, Code) sind, sofern nicht anders gekennzeichnet, urheberrechtlich geschützt. Die Vervielfältigung oder Verwendung in anderen elektronischen oder gedruckten Publikationen ist ohne ausdrückliche Zustimmung nicht gestattet.

thank_you_page.lead: Your message has landed. We'll review it and come back within 4 hours during European business hours, sooner if you reached us before lunch CET.
thank_you_page.next_steps_subtitle: Three things worth doing in the next ten minutes:

steps.title: Browse recent work
steps.body: Real cases, real numbers, no marketing fluff.

hero.h1: Websites on the platform that fits your business — not mine.
hero.lead: I build on whatever's right for the job — WordPress, Shopify, Wix Studio, custom Astro or Next.js. My job is helping you pick the right tool, not selling you a trendy one.

platforms.label: Platforms & stack
platforms.title: I work on every serious platform — and a few less-serious ones, when they fit.
platforms.subtitle: The platform is a tool, not an ideology. The right one for you depends on your team, content workflow, budget and growth horizon — not on what's hot on Twitter this month.
platforms.cta_helper: Not sure which platform fits? Tell me about the project — I'll give an honest recommendation, not a pitch for the most expensive option.

groups.title: Quick & affordable
groups.subtitle: When you need something live fast, with light custom work.

platforms.name: Wix Studio
platforms.when: Premium no-code builder. Polished result without long timelines. Good for fast launches and design-led pages.

platforms.name: Elementor (WordPress)
platforms.when: When you need WordPress flexibility but don't want to write custom theme code. Visual editor, dynamic content, plugin ecosystem.

platforms.name: Plain HTML / CSS / JS
platforms.when: Static one-pagers and microsites where every kilobyte matters. Loads in under a second, hosted free on the edge.

groups.title: Most popular for business
groups.subtitle: Battle-tested platforms that 90% of business sites should be on.

platforms.name: WordPress
platforms.when: The default for content sites that need a real CMS. Huge plugin ecosystem, easy editing, near-infinite flexibility with custom code.

platforms.name: WooCommerce
platforms.when: E-commerce on WordPress when you don't want Shopify's transaction fees. Full ownership, custom checkout, integrates with anything.

platforms.name: Shopify
platforms.when: The best choice for serious e-commerce. Stable, fast, every payment provider integrated, scales without sysadmin work.

groups.title: Performance & growth
groups.subtitle: Modern stacks for sites where speed, SEO and editor autonomy all matter.

platforms.name: Astro
platforms.when: Content sites, landing pages, agency sites where Lighthouse 95+ and great SEO are non-negotiable. Static-first, ships almost no JavaScript.

platforms.name: Next.js
platforms.when: SaaS dashboards, app-like interfaces, sites with heavy interactivity. Best-in-class React framework with full server-side power.

platforms.name: Headless CMS
platforms.when: Sanity, Storyblok or Contentful. When the content team needs full editing autonomy without being limited by the front-end stack.

groups.title: Custom solutions
groups.subtitle: When your needs go beyond any off-the-shelf platform.

platforms.name: Node.js bespoke
platforms.when: Custom integrations, internal tools, non-standard logic, real-time features. Built from scratch when no platform fits.

included.title: Discovery & strategy
included.body: We spend the first sprint understanding your business, audience and goals — before deciding on the platform or writing a single line of code.

included.title: Design
included.body: Custom design from scratch, or close collaboration with your in-house designer. Either way, the goal is something that looks like your brand, not a template.

included.title: Engineering
included.body: On the platform we picked together — WordPress, Shopify, Astro, Next.js, custom. Solid code that someone else can also maintain later.

included.title: On-page SEO
included.body: Clean URLs, meta tags, schema, sitemap, hreflang for multilingual, internal linking. Every page is ready for Google before launch.

included.title: Analytics setup
included.body: GA4, Search Console, Microsoft Clarity, conversion goals — connected and tested before the site goes live.

included.title: Handoff & training
included.body: Walk-through video, written docs, your team trained on the admin. 14 days of free bug-fix support after launch.

process.title: Discovery
process.body: Calls with you and your team to understand goals, audience, content needs, integrations. We pick the platform together based on what's right — not on what I want to charge for.

process.title: Design
process.body: Wireframes first, then high-fidelity. Two rounds of revisions baked in. If you bring a designer, I work directly with them on hand-off.

process.title: Build
process.body: Building on the platform we picked. Component library, content integration, performance tuning, QA across browsers and devices.

process.title: Launch & optimize
process.body: Go-live with no downtime. 14 days of fine-tuning based on real traffic, real Search Console data, real user feedback.

deliverables: Lighthouse 95+ across mobile & desktop, where the platform allows it
On-page SEO complete (meta, schema, sitemap, robots, hreflang)
Analytics + conversion tracking wired and tested

tiers_alt_disabled.intro: From $395 for a focused landing page to $11,995+ for a custom SaaS platform. Every project is fixed-price, agreed before work starts — no scope-creep surprises.

cross_link.label: Already have a site?
cross_link.title: If you already have a site, a redesign is usually faster and cheaper than a from-scratch build.

faq.question: Do you work with WordPress / Shopify / Wix?
faq.answer: Yes — all three, and a lot more. WordPress (with custom themes, ACF, Elementor when it fits), Shopify (themes, Liquid, custom apps), Wix Studio for premium no-code projects. The platform is picked based on your needs, not my preference.

faq.question: Can you migrate my site from one platform to another?
faq.answer: Yes — that's actually one of the most common projects. WordPress → Shopify, Wix → Astro, custom → headless CMS. Full SEO preservation through 301 redirects, content migration, redesign or 1:1 rebuild — your call.

faq.question: I have a designer / I need a designer too.
faq.answer: Both work. If you have a designer, I'll integrate cleanly with their hand-off (Figma, Sketch, anything). If you need design too, I do it as part of the project — custom from scratch, no templates.

faq.question: What if I'm not sure which platform fits?
faq.answer: Discovery call is free. Tell me about your business, team, content workflow, growth plans — I'll recommend the platform that gives you the best result for your budget. If your needs fit a $555 Wix project, I'll say so, even if I could build a $11,995 custom one.

faq.question: Do you maintain sites after launch?
faq.answer: Yes — 14 days of bug-fix support are included free. After that, monthly maintenance plans start at $79/month — security updates, content edits, performance monitoring, analytics review. See the Site Support page for full details.

faq.question: Will my site be multilingual-ready?
faq.answer: Every site I build supports multilingual out of the gate — even if you launch with one language. URLs, hreflang, locale routing — wired correctly so adding a new language later is a content task, not a rebuild.

faq.question: Do you handle hosting?
faq.answer: Depends on the platform. Shopify and Wix host themselves. WordPress can go on Cloudflare, SiteGround, Kinsta or your own server. Astro and Next.js deploy to Cloudflare Pages or Vercel by default. I'll set up whatever fits your needs.

faq.question: What happens after launch?
faq.answer: 14 days of free bug-fix support included. After that, monthly maintenance plans start at $79/month — security updates, performance monitoring, content edits, analytics review.

included.body: Annotated specs, component documentation, design tokens exported in code-friendly formats.

faq.question: Do you work in Figma or Sketch?
faq.answer: Figma exclusively. It's the industry standard for collaboration, handoff, and design systems.

faq.question: Do you provide design only or also code?
faq.answer: Both. Design-only handoff or design+code as a unified package — usually 20–30% faster end-to-end than splitting vendors.

faq.question: Can you work with our existing brand?
faq.answer: Yes. We start from your brand guidelines and extend them for the digital context. If guidelines are dated, we flag gaps and propose extensions.

hero.lead: Redesigns that preserve every backlink, every ranking, every conversion path — while making the site faster, clearer, and ready for the next 5 years.

included.title: SEO audit & migration plan
included.body: Full crawl, ranking export, redirect map. Zero traffic loss is the baseline, not a goal.

included.title: UX audit

cross_link.label: Need a brand new site?
cross_link.title: If you don't have a site yet (or the existing one is too far gone to refresh), a from-scratch build is the better path.

faq.question: Will I lose traffic during the redesign?
faq.answer: No. We baseline rankings before launch, build a complete 301 redirect map, run a pre-launch test on a staging URL, and monitor traffic daily for 30 days post-launch. In 12 years we've never lost rankings.

faq.question: Can you keep our existing CMS?
faq.answer: Usually yes. If your CMS is modern (Sanity, Contentful, Strapi, WordPress with proper structure), we redesign on top of it. If it's legacy or fragile, we propose a migration.

faq.question: What about all our existing content?
faq.answer: Everything is audited page-by-page. High-performing content stays. Mid-performing content gets rewritten or merged. Low-performing or duplicate content gets archived with proper redirects.

hero.h1: Predictable organic growth — audited and engineered.
hero.lead: Technical SEO, content strategy, and link building combined into a single quarterly engagement. Every recommendation tied back to revenue.

included.title: Technical audit
included.body: 200+ point crawl: indexation, schema, Core Web Vitals, hreflang, internal linking, redirect chains, JS rendering.

included.title: Keyword strategy
included.body: Primary + cluster keywords per page, search intent mapping, gap analysis vs. top 3 competitors.

included.title: On-page optimization
included.body: Meta, schema, content rewrites, FAQ blocks, internal linking — page by page, prioritized by traffic potential.

included.title: Content production
included.body: Briefs by an SEO strategist, drafts by senior writers, edits by a native speaker. 4–8 articles per month.

included.title: Link building
included.body: Digital PR + niche guest posts. Quality over quantity — 4–8 referring domains per month, DR 40+.

included.title: Reporting
included.body: Monthly: rankings, traffic, conversions, cost per organic lead. Quarterly: strategy review and re-prioritization.

process.title: Audit (month 0)
process.body: Full technical + content audit. Baseline rankings exported. 90-day plan.

process.title: Quick wins (month 1)
process.body: Technical fixes, on-page optimization on top 20 pages. Most clients see ranking lifts within 30 days.

process.title: Content engine (months 2+)
process.body: 4–8 articles per month, each tied to a high-intent keyword cluster.

process.title: Link building & scale
process.body: Outreach campaign starts month 2, ramps to 4–8 referring domains/month by month 3.

deliverables: Keyword strategy doc with monthly priorities

tiers.tagline: Full-service monthly SEO engagement.

tiers.name: Scale
tiers.tagline: Aggressive growth for competitive verticals.

faq.question: How soon will I see results?
faq.answer: Technical fixes show in Search Console within 2–4 weeks. New content typically starts ranking month 3–4. Link-driven authority compounds over months 6–12.

faq.question: Do you guarantee #1 rankings?
faq.answer: No — and you should run from anyone who does. What we guarantee: a measurable plan, monthly progress reports, and full transparency.

faq.question: How is this different from cheap SEO retainers?
faq.answer: Cheap retainers sell volume — 50 spammy backlinks, mass-produced content. We sell outcomes — specific keyword wins, specific traffic targets, with the analytics to prove it.

hero.lead: Full-funnel paid media management. Every campaign tied to a CAC target, every dollar tracked from impression to revenue.

faq.question: What's the minimum ad spend you work with?
faq.answer: $5,000/month for Google or Meta. Below that, the management fee eats too much of the budget. For SaaS or high-LTV businesses we sometimes flex this — ask.

faq.question: Do you charge a percentage of spend?
faq.answer: Yes — a flat retainer covers strategy, tracking, and reporting; a small percentage (6–8%) covers ongoing creative and optimization. This keeps incentives aligned.

faq.question: Can you take over an existing account?
faq.answer: Always. Most accounts we audit waste 20–40% of spend on misconfigured tracking, broken attribution, or stale bid strategies. Migration is part of the kickoff at no extra cost.

faq.question: What sites do you support?
faq.answer: Anything modern (Astro, Next.js, headless CMS) and most WordPress installations under 200 plugins. We don't support drag-and-drop builders (Wix, Squarespace) — they're already managed by their platforms.

faq.question: What if my site goes down?
faq.answer: Monitoring catches outages within 60 seconds. Business hours: response within 30 minutes. Scale tier: 1-hour SLA, 24/7.

faq.question: Can I cancel monthly?
faq.answer: Yes. No long-term contracts. We earn the next month every month.

faq.question: Why Kotlin Multiplatform and not React Native or FlutterFlow?
faq.answer: KMM gives native UI on both iOS and Android from a single Kotlin codebase. React Native ships JavaScript through a bridge — fine, but laggier on complex UIs. FlutterFlow is a no-code builder where you pay monthly to keep your app alive and don't own the source. KMM gives you ownership, performance, and the same architecture used by Cash App and Square.

faq.question: How long does an app actually take?
faq.answer: Trainer/course apps are 3-4 weeks. Booking apps 4-5 weeks. Custom 5-6 weeks. The clock starts after Discovery (week 1), once we have a fixed scope. We demo weekly, so you always know where we are.

faq.question: What's required from me as a client?
faq.answer: Brand assets (logo, colors), final product / service list, store accounts (Apple Developer + Google Play Console — yours, not ours), and decisions on monetization. Roughly 4-6 hours of your time across the project.

faq.question: Will my app be in the App Store and Google Play?
faq.answer: Yes. We handle screenshots, descriptions, store metadata, review responses, and the first production submission to both stores. You keep ownership of both store accounts.

faq.question: Who owns the code?
faq.answer: You do. Source code lives on a GitHub repo we transfer to your account at handoff. No license restrictions, no platform lock-in. If you fire us tomorrow, another developer can pick the code up immediately.

faq.question: What about Apple Developer Account fees?
faq.answer: $99/year (Apple) + $25 one-time (Google). These are paid by you directly to Apple/Google — they belong on accounts you own. We help you set both up if you don't already have them.

faq.question: Can I add features later?
faq.answer: Absolutely. The whole architecture is built around adding features without rewriting. Most clients ship a v1, gather feedback for 2-3 months, then come back for a v2 update.

faq.question: What if Apple rejects my app?
faq.answer: We handle the back-and-forth with the App Review team and resubmit. Common rejections (subscription wording, privacy declarations, missing screenshots) are anticipated and avoided up front. Resubmission is included in the project.

faq.question: Do you handle iOS/Android updates after launch?
faq.answer: That's what the App Support Plan ($149/month) is for — OS compatibility checks, store policy updates, crash monitoring, dependency upgrades. The first 30 days are free; after that, the plan is required because abandoned apps get pulled from the stores.

faq.question: Can the app integrate with my existing website?
faq.answer: Yes. The Supabase backend can connect to a WordPress, Astro, or Next.js front-end, so customers see the same data on web and mobile. Two-way sync is part of every Booking tier.

faq.question: What about user data and privacy?
faq.answer: Supabase is hosted in the EU (Frankfurt by default, Ireland on request). GDPR-compliant by configuration. We add privacy policy text, App Tracking Transparency prompts, and Data Safety declarations as part of every launch.

faq.question: Do you build the backend or use an existing one?
faq.answer: By default we set up a fresh Supabase project — auth, database, storage, realtime, all configured. If you already have a Supabase / Firebase / custom REST backend, we connect to it.

faq.question: Can I see the code before paying full price?
faq.answer: Yes — projects are split into milestones (Discovery, Design+Architecture, Build, Launch). After each milestone we transfer the corresponding code into your GitHub. You never pay for code you haven't seen.

faq.question: What happens if I want to migrate to another developer later?
faq.answer: Hand off the GitHub repo + Supabase project + store accounts (already yours) and any KMM-experienced developer can take over. The stack is mainstream, the code is documented, and there's nothing proprietary holding you to us.

faq.question: How do you handle multilingual apps?
faq.answer: EN / UK / RU is included as a single language at launch (your choice). Additional languages are an add-on at $295 each — translation memory, locale-aware formatting, and store listings localized.

final_cta.title: Ready to ship your app?
final_cta.subtitle: Tell me about your project — I respond within 4 hours. The discovery call is free, even if we don't end up working together.
